tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Google 'Kyoto eyeglasses shops' and open Google Maps
tasks.hook_line: You're already in Kyoto right now—find shops before they close today.
tasks.reward: Locks in where to go tomorrow for the first pair of 4
nuggets: Major chains: Zoff, JINS, Paris Miki
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm currently traveling here (day 12 of 16) and want to buy 4 pairs of glasses during my trip to save money vs US prices. I need stores that can work with a US prescription and ideally offer same-day service. Give me a list of 5-7 shops with addresses, opening hours, and Google Maps links. Include both major chains (Zoff, JINS) and any well-reviewed independent opticians in central Kyoto.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Find insurance card and look up vision coverage number
tasks.hook_line: 4 pairs of glasses in Japan could save $400+ vs US prices—but only if insurance cooperates.
tasks.reward: Confirms you can buy with confidence and file for reimbursement later
nuggets: Most US vision plans reimburse international purchases
Need itemized receipt with frame/lens breakdown
tasks.system_prompt: I'm in Japan and want to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses to save money vs US prices. I need to validate two things: (1) Can I use my US prescription at Japanese optical shops, and what format do they need? (2) Will my US vision insurance reimburse me for international eyeglasses purchases, and what documentation do I need to collect? Give me a checklist of what to ask the insurance company and what to request from the Japanese shop at time of purchase.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples walking route'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples—which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning—only Takayama and Tokyo left
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for the remaining days in Kyoto (April 5-7, currently here now). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. We've already done Osaka and Nara earlier in the trip.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Takayama day trips hiking' and open results
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is mountain town Japan—totally different vibe from Kyoto's temples.
tasks.reward: Gets mountain/nature portion of trip planned before arrival
nuggets: Shirakawa-go day trip 50min bus each way
Local Hida beef specialty worth trying
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2 days in Takayama, Japan (April 8-9). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like hiking, outdoor spaces, walking, and good food. Looking for a mix of the historic old town, nearby nature/mountain day trips, and local food experiences. Include opening hours, transportation details, and Google Maps links. We're coming from Kyoto and then heading to Tokyo after.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Tokyo neighborhoods to explore'
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is the finale—plan this and the whole trip is mapped.
tasks.reward: Completes all activity planning for the 16-day trip
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 5 days in Tokyo (April 10-14). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like walking, neighborhoods with character, good food, shopping (especially fountain pens and stationery), and unique experiences. Already planning to buy eyeglasses and possibly a watch during the trip. I want a day-by-day framework with neighborhood clusters, must-see spots, and food recommendations. Include opening hours and Google Maps links. We're ending a 16-day trip, so balance energy—not trying to sprint through everything.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open it in VS Code
tasks.hook_line: Developer portals are supposed to make life easier—can you crack the local setup?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work and gets project active again
nuggets: Parked since Feb, needs local environment
tasks.system_prompt: I'm trying to get Cerebro running locally. It's a developer portal for engineers at my company. I haven't touched it since February and it's currently parked because I couldn't get the local environment working. Walk me through the typical setup process for an internal developer portal (likely Node/React/Docker stack). What are the most common blockers for getting these running locally, and how do I debug them? I need a methodical checklist to get from 'git clone' to 'running on localhost.'

recommended_slots.reason: Weekend + ramp_up energy + already in Kyoto = perfect time for quick local research
Medium effort research fits ramp_up energy, closes out current location planning
Low effort admin task, unblocks glasses shopping with quick insurance validation
